sources.name: Oracle's Elixir professional match data
provides: professional champion picks and role assignments
patch, league, team, player, and date context
limitations: many historical player rows have partial performance statistics
does not define subjective champion playstyle labels
recorded game time is not the fantasy roster-lock time

sources.name: Riot Data Dragon
provides: official static tags, spell text, and base statistics
limitations: static tags are not professional role assignments
spell text does not directly establish a pro-play archetype
versions must be mapped carefully to tournament patches

sources.name: Official Riot patch notes
provides: item, rune, system, and objective changes
limitations: publication date is not necessarily the date a pro league adopts the patch
hotfixes and tournament-specific exceptions need separate handling

sources.name: Project champion-role taxonomy
provides: multi-label playstyles such as engage, poke, dive, peel, and scaling
role-specific blind-pick, counter-pick, flex, and execution labels
limitations: contains judgment rather than purely observed facts
must be versioned by role and patch
every label needs provenance, confidence, and review status

sources.name: Riot Match-V5 KR and EUW high-elo matches
provides: high-ranked solo-queue champion usage and outcomes
possible early signals before professional adoption
limitations: requires an API key and rate-limit-aware collection
ranked matches do not reproduce coordinated professional drafts
ban data and inferred roles require validation
